text.title: python-poetry-setup Core Rules
text.summary: Eight testable rules for reproducible Poetry setup.

rule.statement: Always commit poetry.lock. There is no situation where a service or library should omit it from version control.
rule.rationale: Without lockfile, every install regenerates; reproducibility lost.

rule.statement: Run `poetry install --sync --no-interaction` in CI. --sync removes packages that left the lockfile, preventing zombie deps.
rule.rationale: Without --sync, removed deps stay in the venv and silently keep working.

rule.statement: Never run `poetry update` without arguments in a shared repo. Use `poetry update <package>` to bump one package.
rule.rationale: Bare update rewrites the entire lockfile, overwriting other devs/agents' staged resolution.

rule.statement: Split dependencies into groups: main, dev, test, docs. Mark docs `optional = true`.
rule.rationale: Production installs stay minimal; docs/test deps don't bloat the image.

rule.statement: Use tight Python version constraints (`python = "^3.11"`), not broad ranges like `>=3.8`.
rule.rationale: Broad ranges slow the solver and produce hostile conflict messages.

rule.statement: Before any `poetry add`/`poetry remove`, run `poetry check --lock`. Resolve lockfile drift first.
rule.rationale: Adding deps on a dirty lockfile compounds drift; check first.

rule.statement: Docker: builder stage runs `poetry export`; final stage runs `pip install -r requirements.txt`. Never `poetry install` in the final stage.
rule.rationale: Final stage avoids shipping the Poetry runtime; image stays small and faster.

rule.statement: Use snok/install-poetry@v1 in CI and cache .venv keyed by OS + Python version + poetry.lock hash.
rule.rationale: Reduces install time from minutes to seconds on cache hit.

rule.statement: Lockfile committed, --sync in CI, dep groups, tight Python constraint, multi-stage Docker, .venv cached.
Project uses uv/hatch/Rye or has no migration mandate from requirements.txt.
